title: La pasta e i suoi sughi
grammar_features_introduced: conjugated Italian present tense (è, ha, aderiscono, cuoce, usa)
receptive passato prossimo with essere (è cotta, è diffuso)
comparative constructions: la più semplice, un minuto prima
Italian relative pronouns (che, a cui) in context
quantifier phrases: più di trecento, un mestolo di, una resistenza
contrast between transitive and intransitive cooking verbs (cuocere vs cucinare)
cognate_patterns_showcased: fr -ence / it -enza (résistance / resistenza — via the French gloss)
fr -tion / it -zione (cuisson / cottura — noun pair; cottura itself is -tura not -zione, flagged as non-pattern)
notes: First A2-B1 lesson in the cooking chapter. Italian share rises substantially — now carrying grammatical weight, not just content nouns. French does the heavy lifting of quantifiers and connective tissue. English recedes to clarifying glosses.
